content_en:
"to the righteous soul will he said:) O (thou) soul, in (complete) rest and satisfaction!" (Al-Fajr: 27) Abu Bakr said, "What a good thing this is, O Messenger of Allah!" To this the Prophet said, "The Angel will say it to you when you are dying." (Reported by Ibn Haatim and Abu Nu’ aym.)

Anas, may Allah be pleased with him, quotes the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, as saying: "/t is

incumbent upon my nation to love Abu Bakr and to show gratitude to him." (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

On the authority of ~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn ~Awf, may Allah be pleased with him, who reports that he heard Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, saying: "Abu Bakr will be admitted to Paradise, so will “Umar, so will ‘Uthmaan, so will ~Ali, so will Talhah, so will Az-Zubayr, so will ~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn “Awf, so will Sa*d Ibn Abi Waqqaas, so will Sa*eed Ibn Zayd, so will Abu ‘Ubayd Ibn Al-Jarraah." (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi.)

Abu Moosa_ Al-Ash’aryy narrates: "One day, I performed ablution (Wudoo') in my house and then went out with the determination that I would remain with Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, and spend the whole day with him." I went to the Mosque and asked about the Prophet, peace and blessing be upon him. They (the Companions) told me that he had gone in a certain direction. So I followed the road he took asking about him. I found him by a well called Arees. I sat by its gate, which was made of date-palm leaves, until the Prophet answered the call of nature and then performed Wudoo'.'' I went up to him and he was sitting in the middle of its edge with his shanks uncovered and his legs dangling in the well. I saluted him and went back and sat at the gate. I said, "Today I will be the gatekeeper of Allah's Messenger." Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, came and knocked the door, and I asked, ‘Who is it?' He said, ‘Abu Bakr.’ I said, ‘Wait, please,’ and I went in and said, 'O Messenger of Allah, here is Abu Bakr seeking permission to enter. The Prophet said, "Bid him come in and give him the glad tidings that he will be admitted to Paradise." so IT went out and said to Abu Bakr, 'Come in, and Allah's Messenger gives you the glad tidings that you will be admitted to Paradise.’ Abu Bakr entered and_ sat on the right side of

Allah's Messenger by the edge of the well and hung his legs in the well as the Prophet did and uncovered his legs. | then returned and sat (at the gate). | had left my brother while he was performing Wudoo' and intending to follow me. So I said to myself: ‘If Allah wishes good for so-and-so, i.e. my brother. He will bring him here, Suddenly someone moved the door, and I asked, “Who is it?" He said, ~Umar Ibn AlKhattab, I asked him to wait. Then I went to Allah's Messenger, greeted him, and said, ‘Here is “Umar Ibn AlKhattaab seeking permission to get in.' The Prophet said: "Admit him, and give him the glad tidings that he will be admitted to Paradise.” 1 went back to ~ Umar, bade him come in, and said to him, ‘Allah's Messenger gives you the glad tidings that you will be admitted to Paradise.’ So he entered and sat down with Allah's Messenger on his left side and dangled his legs in the well..." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem)

Abi Mulaykah narrates: I heard Ibn ~ Abbaas say: When “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab was placed in the bier, the people gathered around him praising him and supplicated for him before burial, and I was present. Nothing caught my attention but a person who gripped my shoulder from behind. 1 turned and found that he was “Ali Ibn Abi Taalib. He invoked Allah's Mercy upon “Umar and said: "You have left none behind you (whose) deeds (are so enviable) that I love to meet Allah with By Allah, | hoped that Allah would keep you and your two Companions together. I had often heard Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, say: "J came and there came too Abu Bakr and “Umar; entered and there entered too Abu Bakr and ‘Umar; I went out and there went out too Abu Bakr and “Umar, and I hope and think that Allah will keep you along with them.” (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem.)

Abu Bakr narrates: The Prophet once asked, "Did any of you see anything in his dream?” A man said to the Prophet, "O Messenger of Allah, I saw in my dream as if a balance came down from Heaven in which you were weighed against Abu Bakr and outweighed him, then Abu Bakr was weighed against ~Umar and outweighed him, then “Umar was weighed against ~Uthmaan and outweighed him, then the balance was raised up." (Reported by Abu Dawood and Al-Tirmidhi)

Hadiths on the Virtues of Abu Bakr together with ~Umar:

In addition to the praise of Allah, Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, received from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, alone, the following Hadiths make mention of Abu Bakr together with ~ Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab.

On the authority of Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, who says that he heard Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, say: "While a shepherd was tending his sheep, a wolf attacked them and took away one sheep. When the shepherd chased the wolf, the latter turned towards him and said, 'Who will be its guard on the "day of wild beasts", when there will be no shepherd for it except me! And while a man was driving a cow with a load on it, it turned towards him and spoke to him saying, 'I have not been created for this purpose (i.e. carrying); | have been creating for ploughing. The people exclaimed, 'Glory be to Allah! The Prophet said, "But I believe the story and so do Abu Bakr and ‘Umar. The narrator said, "Abu Bakr and “Umar were not present then." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem.) The Prophet said that Abu Bakr and ‘Umar would believe the story, even though they were not present then, because he knew that they were staunch believers who used to believe

every word of the Prophet.

Abu Sa*eed Al-Khudryy reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "No Prophet was sent except that he had two ministers from the Heaven and two on earth. As for my ministers from Heaven, they are Gabriel (Jibreel) and Michael (Mikaa'eel). And as for my ministers on earth, they are Abu Bakr and ~Umar." (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi.)

Sa*eed Ibn Zayd reports that he heard Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, say: "Abu Bakr will be admitted to Paradise, and so will be “Umar, “Uthmaan," and he mentioned the rest of the ten (people whom the Prophet gave the glad tidings of entering Paradise)." (Reported by the authors of Sunan and others.)

~Umar narrates: The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, went out one day and entered the mosque with Abu Bakr and ~“Umar on his right and left and each holding one hand. Then he said, "This way we will he resurrected on the Day of Judgment. " (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi and AlHaakim.)

Ibn ~Umar also reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "J am the first to he resurrected on the Day of Judgment, followed by Abu Bakr and “Umar afterwards. " (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi and Al-Haakim.)

Ibn ~Umar further reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, once saw Abu Bakr and ~ Umar and said, "They are to me like my hearing and seeing." I (Reported by At-Tabaraani.)

On the authority of Al-Baraa'’ Ibn ~ Aazib who says: I was sitting with the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him,

when Abu Bakr and *Umar came. The Prophet said to them, “All praise and thanks are due to Allah, Who grants me strength through you." (Reported by At-Tabaraani.)

*Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Ghanam narrates, the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said to Abu Bakr and “Umar, "If you both agreed on something, I would not oppose you. " (Reported by Ahmad.)

Ibn Sa*d_ narrates: Ibn “Umar was asked, "Who used to give Fatwa (Islamic legal ruling) during the lifetime of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him?" He said, "Abu Bakr and * Umar, and no one else."

Ibn Mas*ood narrates that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said, "Every Prophet had an intimate companion from amongst his people, and my intimate Companions are Abu Bakr and ~Umar." (Reported by AtTabaraani.)

“Ali narrates that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said; "May Allah rest the soul of Abu Bakr in peace: he gave his daughter (i.e. “Aa'ishah) in marriage to me, carried me (on his riding animal) to the place of Hijrah (i. e. Madeenah,) and freed Bilaal. May Allah rest the soul of “Umar in peace: he spoke the truth even if he were to suffer on account of it, and he observed the truth which made people hate him and caused his loss of friends. May Allah rest the soul of ~Uthmaan in peace: the angels feel shy in his presence. May Allah rest the soul of “Ali in peace: 0 Allah, make him observe the truth wherever he may be. " (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

Sahl is reported to have said: When the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, returned from the Farewell

Pilgrimage (Hajjatul-Wadaa'), he sat in the pulpit, gave praise and thanks to Allah, and then said: "O people, Abu Bakr never did anything that displeased me, so acknowledge this virtue of his. O people, I am pleased with him, ~Umar, ‘Uthmaan, “Ali, Tathah, Az-Zubayr, Sa*d, ~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn “Awf, the earliest Muhajireen, so bear this in mind when dealing with them.” (Reported by At-Tabaraani.)

In a Marfoo* Hadith’, Anas reports that the Prophet,

peace and blessings be upon him, said, "To love Abu Bakr and “Umar is an act of belief, and to hate them is an act of disbelief." (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

The Prophet's Companions and Early Muslims Speak of Abu Bakr:

~Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, may Allah be pleased with him, said, "Abu Bakr is our master." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.) He also said: “If Abu Bakr's faith was placed in one of the scales of a balance and the faith of all the people in the other, his would outweigh theirs." (Reported by Al-Bayhaqyy.)
content_id:
"kepada jiwa yang saleh akan dikatakan:) Wahai jiwa yang tenang! (dalam ketenangan dan kepuasan yang sempurna!)" (Al-Fajr: 27) Abu Bakr berkata, "Alangkah baiknya hal ini, wahai Rasulullah!" Maka Nabi bersabda, "Malaikat akan mengatakannya kepadamu ketika engkau sedang meninggal dunia." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Haatim dan Abu Nu'aym.)

Anas, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Wajib atas umatku untuk mencintai Abu Bakr dan menunjukkan rasa syukur kepadanya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn 'Asaakir.)

Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdur-Rahman Ibn 'Awf, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang menyatakan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Abu Bakr akan dimasukkan ke dalam surga, begitu juga 'Umar, begitu juga 'Utsman, begitu juga 'Ali, begitu juga Thalhah, begitu juga Az-Zubair, begitu juga 'Abdur-Rahman Ibn 'Awf, begitu juga Sa'd Ibn Abi Waqqash, begitu juga Sa'eed Ibn Zaid, begitu juga Abu 'Ubaid Ibn Al-Jarrah." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Tirmidzi.)

Abu Musa Al-Asy'ari meriwayatkan: "Suatu hari, aku berwudhu di rumahku lalu keluar dengan tekad untuk tetap bersama Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan menghabiskan sepanjang hari bersamanya." Aku pergi ke masjid dan bertanya tentang Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Para sahabat memberitahuku bahwa beliau telah pergi ke arah tertentu. Maka aku mengikuti jalan yang beliau tempuh sambil bertanya tentangnya. Aku menemukan beliau di dekat sebuah sumur bernama Arees. Aku duduk di pintunya, yang terbuat dari daun kurma, sampai Nabi selesai buang hajat dan kemudian berwudhu. Aku menghampirinya dan beliau sedang duduk di tengah tepi sumur dengan betis terbuka dan kedua kakinya terjuntai ke dalam sumur. Aku memberi salam kepadanya lalu kembali dan duduk di pintu gerbang. Aku berkata, "Hari ini aku akan menjadi penjaga pintu Rasulullah." Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, datang dan mengetuk pintu, dan aku bertanya, 'Siapa itu?' Ia menjawab, 'Abu Bakr.' Aku berkata, 'Tunggu sebentar,' lalu aku masuk dan berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, ini Abu Bakr meminta izin untuk masuk.' Nabi bersabda, "Persilakan dia masuk dan sampaikan kabar gembira kepadanya bahwa ia akan dimasukkan ke dalam surga." Maka aku keluar dan berkata kepada Abu Bakr, 'Masuklah, dan Rasulullah menyampaikan kabar gembira kepadamu bahwa engkau akan dimasukkan ke dalam surga.' Abu Bakr masuk dan duduk di sisi kanan

Rasulullah di tepi sumur, menjuntaikan kakinya ke dalam sumur sebagaimana Nabi lakukan dan membuka betisnya. Kemudian aku kembali dan duduk (di pintu gerbang). Aku telah meninggalkan saudaraku ketika ia sedang berwudhu dan berniat mengikutiku. Maka aku berkata dalam hati: 'Jika Allah menghendaki kebaikan bagi si fulan, yaitu saudaraku, Dia akan membawanya ke sini.' Tiba-tiba seseorang menggerakkan pintu, dan aku bertanya, 'Siapa itu?' Ia menjawab, ''Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab.' Aku memintanya menunggu. Lalu aku pergi kepada Rasulullah, memberi salam, dan berkata, 'Ini 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab meminta izin untuk masuk.' Nabi bersabda: "Persilakan dia masuk, dan sampaikan kabar gembira kepadanya bahwa ia akan dimasukkan ke dalam surga." Aku kembali kepada 'Umar, mempersilakannya masuk, dan berkata kepadanya, 'Rasulullah menyampaikan kabar gembira kepadamu bahwa engkau akan dimasukkan ke dalam surga.' Maka ia masuk dan duduk bersama Rasulullah di sisi kirinya dan menjuntaikan kakinya ke dalam sumur..." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Abi Mulaykah meriwayatkan: Aku mendengar Ibn 'Abbas berkata: Ketika 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab diletakkan di atas usungan, orang-orang berkumpul di sekelilingnya memujinya dan mendoakannya sebelum pemakaman, dan aku hadir di sana. Tidak ada yang menarik perhatianku selain seseorang yang memegang bahuku dari belakang. Aku berbalik dan mendapati bahwa ia adalah 'Ali Ibn Abi Thalib. Ia memohonkan rahmat Allah untuk 'Umar dan berkata: "Engkau tidak meninggalkan seorang pun di belakangmu (yang) amal perbuatannya (begitu patut ditiru) sehingga aku ingin berjumpa Allah dengannya. Demi Allah, aku berharap Allah menyatukan engkau dengan kedua sahabatmu. Sungguh aku sering mendengar Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Aku datang dan datang pula Abu Bakr dan 'Umar; aku masuk dan masuk pula Abu Bakr dan 'Umar; aku keluar dan keluar pula Abu Bakr dan 'Umar, dan aku berharap serta berpikir bahwa Allah akan menyatukan engkau bersama mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Abu Bakr meriwayatkan: Nabi suatu kali bertanya, "Apakah ada di antara kalian yang melihat sesuatu dalam mimpinya?" Seorang laki-laki berkata kepada Nabi, "Wahai Rasulullah, aku melihat dalam mimpiku seolah-olah sebuah timbangan turun dari langit, engkau ditimbang melawan Abu Bakr dan engkau lebih berat darinya, lalu Abu Bakr ditimbang melawan 'Umar dan Abu Bakr lebih berat darinya, lalu 'Umar ditimbang melawan 'Utsman dan 'Umar lebih berat darinya, kemudian timbangan itu diangkat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud dan Al-Tirmidzi.)

Hadits-hadits tentang Keutamaan Abu Bakr Bersama 'Umar:

Selain pujian dari Allah, Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, juga menerima pujian dari Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, secara pribadi. Hadits-hadits berikut menyebutkan Abu Bakr bersama 'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab.

Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang berkata bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Ketika seorang penggembala sedang menjaga dombanya, seekor serigala menyerang mereka dan mengambil seekor domba. Ketika penggembala itu mengejar serigala tersebut, serigala itu berbalik kepadanya dan berkata, 'Siapa yang akan menjaganya pada "hari binatang-binatang buas", ketika tidak ada penggembala untuknya selain aku!' Dan ketika seorang laki-laki sedang menuntun seekor sapi yang membawa beban, sapi itu berbalik kepadanya dan berbicara kepadanya dengan berkata, 'Aku tidak diciptakan untuk tujuan ini (yaitu membawa beban); aku diciptakan untuk membajak.' Orang-orang berseru, 'Subhanallah!' Nabi bersabda, "Tetapi aku mempercayai cerita itu, begitu juga Abu Bakr dan 'Umar." Perawi berkata, "Abu Bakr dan 'Umar tidak hadir saat itu." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.) Nabi menyatakan bahwa Abu Bakr dan 'Umar akan mempercayai cerita tersebut, meskipun mereka tidak hadir saat itu, karena beliau mengetahui bahwa keduanya adalah orang-orang beriman yang teguh yang senantiasa mempercayai

setiap perkataan Nabi.

Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Tidaklah seorang Nabi diutus kecuali ia memiliki dua menteri dari langit dan dua di bumi. Adapun menteri-menteriku dari langit, mereka adalah Jibril dan Mikail. Dan adapun menteri-menteriku di bumi, mereka adalah Abu Bakr dan 'Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Tirmidzi.)

Sa'eed Ibn Zaid meriwayatkan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Abu Bakr akan dimasukkan ke dalam surga, begitu juga 'Umar, 'Utsman," dan beliau menyebutkan sepuluh orang lainnya (yang diberi kabar gembira oleh Nabi akan masuk surga)." (Diriwayatkan oleh para penulis kitab Sunan dan lainnya.)

'Umar meriwayatkan: Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, keluar suatu hari dan memasuki masjid bersama Abu Bakr dan 'Umar di kanan dan kirinya, masing-masing memegang satu tangan beliau. Kemudian beliau bersabda, "Seperti inilah kami akan dibangkitkan pada Hari Kiamat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Tirmidzi dan Al-Hakim.)

Ibn 'Umar juga meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Aku adalah orang pertama yang dibangkitkan pada Hari Kiamat, diikuti oleh Abu Bakr dan 'Umar setelahnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Tirmidzi dan Al-Hakim.)

Ibn 'Umar lebih lanjut meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, suatu kali melihat Abu Bakr dan 'Umar lalu bersabda, "Mereka berdua bagiku seperti pendengaranku dan penglihatanku." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabarani.)

Diriwayatkan dari Al-Bara' Ibn 'Azib yang berkata: Aku sedang duduk bersama Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam,

ketika Abu Bakr dan 'Umar datang. Nabi bersabda kepada mereka, "Segala puji dan syukur hanyalah milik Allah, yang memberiku kekuatan melalui kalian berdua." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabarani.)

'Abdur-Rahman Ibn Ghanam meriwayatkan, Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda kepada Abu Bakr dan 'Umar, "Jika kalian berdua sepakat tentang sesuatu, aku tidak akan menentang kalian." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad.)

Ibn Sa'd meriwayatkan: Ibn 'Umar ditanya, "Siapa yang biasa memberikan fatwa pada masa hidup Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam?" Ia menjawab, "Abu Bakr dan 'Umar, dan tidak ada orang lain."

Ibn Mas'ud meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, "Setiap Nabi memiliki sahabat karib dari kalangan umatnya, dan sahabat-sahabat karibku adalah Abu Bakr dan 'Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabarani.)

'Ali meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Semoga Allah merahmati Abu Bakr: ia menikahkan putrinya (yaitu 'Aisyah) kepadaku, membawaku (di atas kendaraannya) ke tempat hijrah (yaitu Madinah), dan membebaskan Bilal. Semoga Allah merahmati 'Umar: ia berbicara kebenaran meskipun harus menderita karenanya, dan ia menjaga kebenaran yang membuat orang-orang membencinya dan menyebabkannya kehilangan teman-teman. Semoga Allah merahmati 'Utsman: para malaikat merasa malu di hadapannya. Semoga Allah merahmati 'Ali: Ya Allah, jadikanlah ia senantiasa bersama kebenaran di mana pun ia berada." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn 'Asaakir.)

Diriwayatkan dari Sahl, ia berkata: Ketika Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, kembali dari Haji

Wada' (Hajjatul-Wadaa'), beliau duduk di atas mimbar, memuji dan bersyukur kepada Allah, lalu bersabda: "Wahai manusia, Abu Bakr tidak pernah melakukan sesuatu yang membuatku tidak ridha, maka akuilah keutamaan ini darinya. Wahai manusia, aku ridha terhadap Abu Bakr, 'Umar, 'Utsman, 'Ali, Thalhah, Az-Zubayr, Sa'd, 'Abdur-Rahman Ibn 'Awf, dan kaum Muhajirin yang terdahulu, maka ingatlah hal ini ketika kalian berinteraksi dengan mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabarani.)

Dalam sebuah hadith Marfu', Anas meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi,

shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, "Mencintai Abu Bakr dan 'Umar adalah bagian dari iman, dan membenci keduanya adalah bagian dari kekufuran." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Para Sahabat Nabi dan Kaum Muslimin Awal Berbicara tentang Abu Bakr:

'Umar Ibn Al-Khaththab, radhiyallahu 'anhu, berkata, "Abu Bakr adalah pemimpin kami." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.) Beliau juga berkata: "Seandainya iman Abu Bakr diletakkan di salah satu sisi timbangan dan iman seluruh manusia di sisi yang lain, niscaya imannya akan lebih berat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bayhaqi.)
content_ar: "to the الصالح النفس will قال:) O (thou) النفس, in (complete) rest and satisfaction!" (Al-Fajr: 27) أبو بكر said, "What a الخير thing this is, O Messenger of الله!" To this النبي ﷺ said, "The Angel will say it to you عندما you are dying." (Reported by Ibn Haatim and Abu Nu’ aym.) أنس, may الله be pleased with him, quotes النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, as saying: "/t is incumbent upon my nation to المحبة أبو بكر and to show الشكر to him." (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.) On the authority of ~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn ~Awf, may الله be pleased with him, who reports that he heard الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, saying: "أبو بكر will be admitted to الجنة, لذا will “عمر, لذا will ‘Uthmaan, لذا will ~علي, لذا will Talhah, لذا will Az-Zubayr, لذا will ~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn “Awf, لذا will Sa*d Ibn Abi Waqqaas, لذا will Sa*eed Ibn Zayd, لذا will Abu ‘Ubayd Ibn Al-Jarraah." (Reported by Al-الترمذي.) Abu Moosa_ Al-Ash’aryy narrates: "يوماً ما, I performed ablution (الوضوء') in my house and then went out with the determination that I would remain with الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, and spend the whole day with him." I went to the المسجد and asked about النبي ﷺ, peace and البركة be upon him. They (the الصحابة) told me that he had gone in a certain direction. لذا I followed the road he took asking about him. I found him by a well called Arees. I sat by its gate, which was made of date-palm leaves, حتى النبي ﷺ answered the call of nature and then performed الوضوء'.'' I went up to him and he was sitting in the middle of its edge with his shanks uncovered and his legs dangling in the well. I saluted him and went back and sat at the gate. I said, "Today I will be the gatekeeper of الله's Messenger." أبو بكر, may الله be pleased with him, came and knocked the door, and I asked, ‘Who is it?' قال, ‘أبو بكر.’ I said, ‘Wait, please,’ and I went in and said, 'O Messenger of الله, here is أبو بكر seeking permission to enter. النبي ﷺ said, "Bid him come in and give him the glad tidings that he will be admitted to الجنة." لذا IT went out and said to أبو بكر, 'Come in, and الله's Messenger gives you the glad tidings that you will be admitted to الجنة.’ أبو بكر entered and_ sat on the الحق side of الله's Messenger by the edge of the well and hung his legs in the well as النبي ﷺ did and uncovered his legs. | then returned and sat (at the gate). | had left my الأخ بينما he was performing الوضوء' and intending للاتباع me. لذا I said to myself: ‘إذا الله wishes الخير for لذا-and-لذا, i.e. my الأخ. He will bring him here, Suddenly someone moved the door, and I asked, “Who is it?" قال, ~عمر Ibn AlKhattab, I asked him to wait. Then I went to الله's Messenger, greeted him, and said, ‘Here is “عمر Ibn AlKhattaab seeking permission to get in.' النبي ﷺ said: "Admit him, and give him the glad tidings that he will be admitted to الجنة.” 1 went
